extraction_reasoning: The litigation tables distinguish matters By the Company/Subsidiaries from matters Against them: I counted only Against rows, with criminal proceedings of 3 against the Company, nil against Subsidiaries, 1 against Directors and 1 against Promoters; statutory/regulatory proceedings are nil in every Against row; and tax proceedings are 10 against the Company plus 5 against Subsidiaries, excluding promoter/director tax matters and all By rows. For quantified matters against the Company and Subsidiaries, I used the table amounts of ₹1,535.23 million and ₹52.94 million, respectively, and added the separately disclosed ₹19.63 million of consumer proceedings and ₹94.16 million pre-litigation mediation against the Company, totaling ₹1,701.96 million, or ₹170.196 crore; unquantified matters were excluded. The contingent-liabilities disclosure is as of June 30, 2024 and has no stated Total row, so the disclosed ₹4.14 million other income-tax matter plus ₹1.83 million paid under protest was summed to ₹5.97 million, or ₹0.597 crore; the FY 2016-17 income-tax matter is shown as nil. No company-wide top-five or top-ten customer concentration percentage is disclosed; the percentages disclosed relate to Financial Services/credit-partner concentration rather than customers. Litigation and contingent-liability tables use ₹ million, converted to crore by dividing by 10.
